frameworks.name: nativist framework for AI
frameworks.one_line: innate structure as the route to general intelligence

frameworks.name: empiricist framework for AI
frameworks.one_line: intelligence emerging from experience and learning

frameworks.name: rich mental models view
frameworks.one_line: humans reason via structured internal world models

frameworks.name: mind-is-flat view
frameworks.one_line: cognition as shallow improvisation rather than deep models

core_question: What makes a generally intelligent agent, and how should nativist and empiricist views of AI be reconciled?
key_assumptions: general intelligence is a meaningful, unitary target
safe AI requires shared worldview with humans
interpretations: nativist and empiricist frameworks agree more than they disagree
general intelligence requires generalization of both concepts and programs
artificial intelligibility is needed for safe and useful general AI
out_of_scope_note: Synthesis book chapter, not an empirical paper; no findings to extract.
